title: Tel
description: Tel is a field-specialized wrapper around the shared input foundation with native `type="tel"`, built-in clear affordance, and a phone-oriented action icon.
intro: Use `ui-tel` for customer contact fields, support callbacks, escalation paths, and any workflow that captures phone numbers. It inherits the shared field API for labels, help text, size, variant, readonly, disabled, and validation messaging while using telephone-friendly input semantics.

featureSections.title: Basic telephone fields
featureSections.description: Use the default field for direct contact numbers and let the built-in clear action handle quick replacements when the number is already populated.

featureSections.title: Layout, size, and variant
featureSections.description: Telephone fields follow the same density and label rhythm as the rest of the field family, so they can fit compact admin forms and larger support flows without special-case styling.

featureSections.title: Readonly, disabled, and inline error states
featureSections.description: Readonly works for visible but locked contact details, disabled removes interaction completely, and lightweight inline formatting checks can surface before form submission.

featureSections.title: Validation with reactive forms
featureSections.description: When a phone number is required for verification or workflow routing, use reactive-form validation so the field behaves consistently with the rest of the form.

featureSections.title: Contact routing form
featureSections.description: A practical pattern for admin or profile screens: group primary, escalation, and after-hours numbers in one contact surface with a compact value summary.

featureSections.title: Callback request panel
featureSections.description: A stronger end-to-end example: collect the preferred number, keep service-window context visible, and place the next action directly beside the field.

accessibility.title: Label and name
accessibility.body: Prefer a visible `label` so the phone number's purpose is explicit, especially when multiple contact fields appear together. Help and error text contribute through `aria-describedby`.

accessibility.title: Native telephone semantics
accessibility.body: The inner input uses native `type="tel"`, which helps mobile browsers show phone-friendly keyboards and aligns the field with browser autofill expectations. When `autocomplete` is omitted, the inner input falls back to `tel`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard and actions
accessibility.body:
This is a standard text-entry field backed by a native telephone input. Clear and call affordances are exposed in the field chrome when the field is editable.

| Aspect | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| focus | native tel input receives focus |
| typing | native tel input behavior |
| clear action | appears only when the field has a value and is editable |
| call action | stays visible while editable to reinforce the contact purpose |
| readonly / disabled | actions are hidden and direct editing is blocked |

apiSections.title: TelComponent - ui-tel
apiSections.lead: Standalone telephone field built on the shared field base. It keeps the same layout, validation, and form-control model as the other field components while adding phone-oriented input semantics and actions.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Hint shown when the number is empty.
Marks the native input as required.
Keeps the number visible but not editable.
Optional autocomplete hint. The inner input falls back to `tel` when omitted.
Visual treatment of the input surface.

paragraphs: The component extends the shared `FieldComponent`, so CVA integration, focus handling, described-by wiring, and validation behavior match the rest of the field family.
Clear appears only when a value is present, while the call icon remains visible for editable fields to reinforce that the field captures a phone number rather than a generic text string.
